title: Oligo(dT) Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization for Poly(A) mRNAs in Drosophila Tissues
author: Ankur Kumar
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: Dextran sulfate (Merck, catalog number: 265152-M) Note: Dextran sulfate is a highly hydrated polysaccharide that acts as an effective volume exclusion agent. It affects the probe concentration by preventing its solubilization in water and thus gives higher hybridization rates in aqueous solutions.

ProLong TM Gold antifade mountant (Thermo, catalog number: P10114 ) Note: ProLong TM Gold antifade mountant is a commercially available glycerol-based mounting media supplemented with an antifading agent that protects the fluorescent signal intensity.
Transparent Nail polish (Shade-40, Lakme, Model No TWUY

steps.title: genotype, which enables visualization of the...
steps.instruction: genotype, which enables visualization of the nuclear membrane in the larval salivary gland. Reagents

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: PFA is a polymer with a wide range of monomeric and oligomeric units of formaldehyde. It dissolves in water and depolymerizes into a monomeric form of formaldehyde. Formaldehyde can crosslink cellular constituents, particularly proteins with lysine residues. Formaldehyde is a toxic chemical; therefore, always wear a mask and gloves during handling.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: DEPC irreversibly inactivates RNase contamination that may be present in dH 2 O. It is carcinogenic and, therefore, needs to be handled carefully.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: Yeast tRNA is used as a nonspecific blocking agent, protecting both the probe and cellular RNA.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: RNase inhibitors directly bind with RNases in a non-competitive mode and thus block their activity. They have been widely used to stop the activity of RNases that are present within the cell. The presence of 1 U/μL RNase inhibitor can block most of the RNase activity during FISH.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: Formamide interferes with hydrogen bonding in double-stranded polynucleotides and thus destabilizes double-strand formation. The presence of formamide in hybridization buffer, therefore, reduces the melting and annealing temperature, which keeps the tissue morphology relatively intact due to low incubation temperature. The working concentration of formamide in hybridization buffer varies from 10% to 50%, depending upon the probe length and its GC content.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: DAPI is a fluorescent stain that binds to the AT-rich region of DNA, with excitation at 405 nm and emission spectrum between 420 and 480 nm (blue color). The fluorescence of DAPI is enhanced several times following its binding with DNA.

steps.title: Riboprobe; probes used here for FISH...
steps.instruction: Riboprobe; probes used here for FISH are single-stranded, non-self-complementary short stretches of polynucleotides (20–1500 nucleotide long DNA or RNA) that have a complementary sequence to the target RNA. In the present study, we used RNase Free HPLC Purified 45 nucleotide long stretch of oligo(dT) probe tagged with Cy3 at the 5′ end. We customized the probe and procured it from Integrated DNA Technologies (IDT) Solutions

steps.title: Reagent Concentration Quantity NaCl 1
steps.instruction: Reagent Concentration Quantity NaCl 1.37 M 80 g KCl 27 mM 2 g Na 2 HPO 4 100 mM 14.4 g KH 2 PO 4 18 mM 2.45 g Take 800 mL of distilled water (dH 2 O) and dissolve all four components. Adjust the pH to 7.4 and make up the volume to 1 L with dH 2 O. Autoclave and store the 10× PBS at room temperature (RT). Dilute the 10× PBS with dH 2 O in a 1:9 ratio to make the 1× PBS as a working solution and store it at 4 °C.

steps.title: Reagent Concentration Quantity NaCl 3 M 175
steps.instruction: Reagent Concentration Quantity NaCl 3 M 175.3 g Sodium citrate 0.3 M 77.4 g Take 800 mL of dH 2 O and dissolve both components. Adjust the pH to 7.0 with HCl and make up the volume to 1 L with dH 2 O. Autoclave and store at RT.

steps.title: 4% PFA Dissolve 4
steps.instruction: 4% PFA Dissolve 4.0 g of PFA in 75 mL of 1× PBS at 60 °C with constant stirring for 10 min. Slowly add 5 N NaOH to the cloudy mixture until a clear solution is formed. Filter the solution with a Whatman filter paper and adjust the pH to 7.0 with HCl or NaOH. Make up the volume to 100 mL with 1× PBS and store in small aliquots at -20 °C. Defrost an aliquot and use it on the same day of the experiment. It is, however, preferred that small volumes of PFA solution be prepared fresh, as required. Prolonged storage of formaldehyde leads to its polymerization in the form of paraformaldehyde, thus reducing its fixative properties.

steps.title: 50% dextran sulfate Slowly dissolve 20...
steps.instruction: 50% dextran sulfate Slowly dissolve 20 g of dextran sulfate powder in 30 mL of DEPC-treated dH 2 O and leave the tube overnight on the rotator. Make up the volume to 40 mL with dH 2 O and store at -20 °C in small aliquots. 50% dextran sulfate is very viscous, and special care should be taken during pipetting.

0.1% DEPC Mix 0.1 mL of DEPC in 100 mL of dH 2 O (0.1% DEPC) and autoclave after 4 h incubation at 37 °C. DEPC treatment is a very effective way to make RNase-free solutions; however, buffers like Tris or HEPES cannot be treated with DEPC as they absorb DEPC and make it unavailable for RNase. To prepare these buffers, DEPC-treated dH 2 O should be used to dissolve the RNase-free reagent powders.

0.3% PBST Triton X-100 is a non-ionic mild detergent that solubilizes proteins and is commonly used to permeabilize the cell membrane. Dissolve 0.03 mL of Triton X-100 in 10 mL of 1× PBS to make 0.3% PBST, which is used as a washing buffer in FISH.

steps.title: 100 mg/mL yeast tRNA Dissolve 25...
steps.instruction: 100 mg/mL yeast tRNA Dissolve 25 mg of yeast tRNA powder in 250 μL of DEPC-treated dH 2 O and store at -20 °C.

steps.title: 1 μg/mL DAPI Dissolve 1 mg...
steps.instruction: 1 μg/mL DAPI Dissolve 1 mg of DAPI in 1 mL of dH 2 O and store the stock (1 mg/mL) solution at -20 °C. Dilute the DAPI stock solution in 1× PBS (1:

steps.title: to make the 1 μg/mL working DAPI solution
steps.instruction: to make the 1 μg/mL working DAPI solution. Store at 4 °C for several weeks.

steps.title: Permeabilization buffer Reagent Concentration Quantity Triton...
steps.instruction: Permeabilization buffer Reagent Concentration Quantity Triton X-100 1% 1 μL NP-40 1% 1 μL 40 U/μL RNase inhibitor 1 U/μL 2.5 μL 1× PBS 95.5 μL Dissolve 1 μL of Triton X-100, 1 μL of NP-40, and 2.5 μL of 40 U/μL RNase-inhibitor in 95.5 μL of 1× PBS. Use fresh.

steps.title: Hybridization buffer Reagent Concentration Quantity Formamide...
steps.instruction: Hybridization buffer Reagent Concentration Quantity Formamide 25% 25 μL 20× SSC 2× 10 μL 50% dextran sulfate 10% 20 μL 100 mg/mL yeast tRNA 1 mg/mL 1 μL 100 ng/mL Oligo(dT) riboprobe 5 ng/mL 5 μL DEPC-treated dH 2 O 39 μL Take 39 μL of DEPC-treated dH 2 O to dissolve 25 μL of formamide, 10 μL of 20× SSC pH 7.0, 20 μL of 50% dextran sulfate, 1 μL of 100 mg/mL yeast tRNA, and 5 μL of 100 ng/μL Cy3-labeled oligo(dT) riboprobe. Use fresh.

steps.title: Wash buffer Reagent Concentration Quantity Formamide...
steps.instruction: Wash buffer Reagent Concentration Quantity Formamide 25% 25 μL 20× SSC 2× 10 μL DEPC-treated dH 2 O 65 μL Dissolve 25 μL of formamide and 10 μL of 20× SSC in 65 μL of DEPC-treated dH 2 O. Use fresh. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Glass slide (Blue Star microslide, 75 mm × 25 mm)
steps.instruction: Glass slide (Blue Star microslide, 75 mm × 25 mm)

steps.title: Glass coverslips (Blue Star, 22 mm2, part no
steps.instruction: Glass coverslips (Blue Star, 22 mm2, part no. CSI-

steps.title: Collect actively wandering, healthy third-instar larvae in a Petri dish
steps.instruction: Collect actively wandering, healthy third-instar larvae in a Petri dish.

steps.title: Wash larvae with distilled water (dH...
steps.instruction: Wash larvae with distilled water (dH 2 O) and dry them on tissue paper.

steps.title: Dissect out the desired internal tissues...
steps.instruction: Dissect out the desired internal tissues of larvae in 1× PBS on a glass slide with the help of fine forceps or dissecting needles.

steps.title: Fix the tissues by incubating them...
steps.instruction: Fix the tissues by incubating them in freshly prepared 4% PFA at RT for 20 min in a microcentrifuge tube. Perform all FISH steps in the same microcentrifuge tube. Note: Fix the tissue immediately after dissection to minimize any stress to the tissue. Ensure that the tissues are properly submerged in the fixing solution and not adhering to the container wall or floating on the surface.

steps.title: Remove the fixative and wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove the fixative and wash the tissues with 200 μL of 0.3% PBST on a rotator for 10 min at RT. Repeat this washing step three times.

steps.title: Incubate the tissue with permeabilization buffer...
steps.instruction: Incubate the tissue with permeabilization buffer for 20 min at 4 °C on a rotator to permeabilize the cell and nuclear membrane.

steps.title: Rinse the tissue with 200 μL of 0
steps.instruction: Rinse the tissue with 200 μL of 0.3% PBST three times for 10 min each.

steps.title: Rinse the tissue further with 200...
steps.instruction: Rinse the tissue further with 200 μL of 2× SSC twice for 10 min each. B. Probe hybridization

steps.title: For each sample of 5–6 larval...
steps.instruction: For each sample of 5–6 larval tissue, prepare ~30 μL of hybridization solution. Thoroughly mix all components with a 200 μL pipette.

steps.title: Incubate the tissue with hybridization solution...
steps.instruction: Incubate the tissue with hybridization solution in a 500 μL microcentrifuge tube at 42 °C for 12–16 h. Make sure that the cap of the tube is properly closed and sealed with parafilm during incubation to prevent drying. Note: The composition of the hybridization buffer and incubation temperature may vary with probe length and its GC content. Smaller probes (~30 nt) need a relatively lower concentration of formamide (10%) and lower incubation temperature (35–37 °C), while longer probes (~500 nt) need a higher concentration of formamide (50%) and higher incubation temperature (60–65 °C).

steps.title: Rinse the tissue at 42 °C...
steps.instruction: Rinse the tissue at 42 °C with freshly prepared prewarmed wash buffer three times for 5 min each.

steps.title: Rinse the tissue again at 42...
steps.instruction: Rinse the tissue again at 42 °C with prewarmed 2× SSC, followed by 1× SSC, three times for 5 min each.

steps.title: Rinse the tissue further with 1×...
steps.instruction: Rinse the tissue further with 1× PBS at RT three times for 5 min each. Note: Immunostaining for a protein of interest can be performed to examine its interaction with poly(A) RNA in the cell. Immunostaining can be performed just after completion of FISH and before counterstaining with DAPI [2,7,15,16]. C. Counterstaining and microscopy

steps.title: Counterstain the nuclei with DAPI (1...
steps.instruction: Counterstain the nuclei with DAPI (1 μg/mL) by incubating the tissue for 10 min at RT. Mount the tissue in antifade mounting media. Store the slides in a dark place at -20 °C until further examination.

steps.title: Examine the fluorescence signals using appropriate...
steps.instruction: Examine the fluorescence signals using appropriate excitation and emission filters under a fluorescence or confocal microscope. Note: The excitation and emission spectra of the fluorochromes used in FISH should be sufficiently different for clear distinction of the two signals (without any bleeding effect).

description: Validation of protocol This protocol has been used and validated in the following research article: Singh et al. [9]. The RNA helicase UPF1 associates with mRNAs co-transcriptionally and is required for the release of mRNAs from gene loci. eLife.
